user_query: Use powershell to set the window style with the title "abc" as a resizable style
response:
Certainly! To achieve this task in PowerShell, you can utilize the Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) to interact with the operating system at a lower level. Below is a PowerShell script that sets the window style for a window with the title "abc" to make it resizable:

```powershell
# Define the window title
$windowTitle = "abc"

# Use WMI to query processes
$processes = Get-WmiObject -Query "SELECT * FROM Win32_Process WHERE Name='notepad.exe' OR Caption LIKE '%$windowTitle%'"

foreach ($process in $processes) {
    # Get the process ID
    $pid = $process.ProcessId

    # Use WMI to get the window handle of the process
    $hwnd = (Get-WmiObject -Query "SELECT WindowTitle, ProcessId, Handle FROM Win32_Window WHERE ProcessId=$pid").Handle

    # Use WMI to change the window style
    Set-WmiInstance -Namespace root\cimv2 -Class Win32_Window -Arguments @{Handle=$hwnd; Style="0x10000000"}  # WS_SIZEBOX flag
}
```
